title: Fix public download links: unguessable 20-character tokens + working public download routing

requirements.text: Replace the current predictable numeric public download tokens (e.g., "/#/d/1") with unique, unguessable 20-character tokens for every file, and invalidate all old numeric tokens so they no longer work.

quotes: it means that the links are like this: /#/d/1 they should really be 20 random characters and unique.
replaced. d/1 are simply not safe.

acceptanceCriteria: After upload finalization, each file’s metadata publicToken is exactly 20 characters long.
Tokens are unique across all stored files; creating many uploads does not produce collisions.
Old numeric tokens (e.g., "1", "2") no longer successfully download files and return a clear error (trap) from downloadFile().
The backend token->file lookup map is consistent with file metadata (i.e., every file’s publicToken resolves to that file, and removed/old tokens do not resolve).

requirements.text: Regenerate and reassign new 20-character tokens for all existing stored files so that previously issued short links are invalidated, and ensure any necessary state upgrade/migration updates both stored metadata and the token index accordingly.

quotes: replaced. d/1 are simply not safe.

acceptanceCriteria: After deploying the fix, existing files that previously had short numeric tokens now have new 20-character tokens.
Previously shared links using old tokens stop working for those existing files.
New links created from the UI (copy link) match the regenerated tokens and successfully download the file.

requirements.text: Fix the public download link format and routing so that opening a copied public link reliably loads the public download route and triggers a working file download.
acceptanceCriteria: The copied public link points to a URL that routes to the PublicDownloadPage ("/d/$token") in the deployed app, not a URL that leaves the app on the home route with an unhandled hash fragment.
Opening the copied link in a new tab shows the PublicDownloadPage and successfully downloads the file via actor.downloadFile(token).
If the token is invalid, the user sees an English error message on the PublicDownloadPage.
The existing Copy Link actions in both the user file list and admin file list produce working links.

requirements.text: Improve the PublicDownloadPage behavior so downloads start automatically when the page loads with a valid token, while still providing a manual Download button as fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: When navigating to "/d/<token>", the page automatically initiates the download once (no infinite retries).
While downloading, the page displays a clear loading state; after triggering the browser download, it displays a success state.
The manual Download button remains available and works if auto-download fails or is cancelled.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor with all logic in backend/main.mo; only add/modify backend/migration.mo if required for state upgrade.

Do not edit frontend immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Use English for any user-facing UI text.
nonGoals: Do not implement or modify file deletion UI/actions in this change (defer delete links/buttons).
Do not add new authentication providers (Internet Identity only).
Do not add external databases or non-Motoko backend services.
